item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is amazing, holy crap. You deserve ever +1 we could give you, this is hilarious!
I genuinely have not been this reactive to a story in so long, thank you so much for this.

Don't stop, believing
Hold on to that feeling
Streetlights, people ~

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded: So I read "Never metafictional character I didn't like", and I was super confused what the other person was supposed to be, then I read this, and I realized it was the typewriter in this story! Now I am a lot less confused, but still completely on board with this absurd concept! +1!

item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post - WikiWalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
This feels weird to me, if only because Never Metafictional Character I Didn't Like is part of a sequence of 'stories' (being the third part); there's a crosslink at the very end to the second part (SCP-3143), which itself contains a crosslink to the Metafictional story.

It seems odd for the first story to link to the third — especially considering that this link comes before the first story's link to the second (which itself contains a link to the third)? Like, this tale refers to Thaddeus Thaum, who hasn't even been introduced yet (he's introduced in SCP-3143, which is linked at the bottom of this article).

item.title: Junior Staff Post - WikiWalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
Edit: author vetoed edits

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ show block
– hide block

I propose linking to the tale Never Metafictional Character I liked because it is a tale featuring this skip (and several other related skips) in action.

Editing this:

I'M SUPPOSED TO BE THE ONE IN CONTROL HERE

To this:

I'M SUPPOSED TO BE THE ONE IN CONTROL HERE

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was laughing at this thing the entire time. The Foundation as a 2-bit villain could only be done like this. And the typewriter was glorious.

Also, someone give fred an oscar for best supporting actor.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love that Murphy let Fred in to play. You just know that he got a kick out of this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This one's amazing.
I wanna read 3143 now but my throat's too scratchy from reading this one. I tried not to, but I don't think it's possible to read it without imitating Murphy's voice…

…maybe that's the real anomaly here?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You added in Fred, you mad genius. I loved it.

item.title: Re: On Murphy Law
item.content:encoded: Loose Canon. I see what you did there and I love it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Honestly. This SCP is a genius composure. A definite upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Absolutely wonderful, definitely one of my favorites (especially when taken with SCP-3143). I really have nothing else to say, this is just gold.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've been lurking this site since 2013 and I can say for sure I've never had a fav skip until now

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Reading this skip - 7/10
Reading this outloud in your best Mid-Atlantic accent with smooth jazz on in the background. 10/10

item.title: Murphy Law: A Deeper Look
item.content:encoded:
Behind the scenes details! In-depth analysis! Get the Blu-Ray commentary of "Type 3403 - FOR MURDER!" at

What is SCP-3043? Sherm's Declassified SCiP Survival Guide
https://youtu.be/BjyvWc0Au7M

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fixed (belatedly) — thanks!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Stellar, absolutely insane. I don't think I've seen a format screw as impressively total since the Minotaur. I loved everything about this. (Though, okay, I'll agree that the anomaly itself being a typewriter was probably the least-great part.)

he'd rather her not get too close.

That should be 'she'.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh, wow — I really appreciate it; this post made my day — thank you! xD

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've been around this site for years (a year or two prior to 2013 I think) and I've read every SCP article on this site (even the constant stream of new articles that pop up and get deleted almost every day). This isn't a brag, I'm sure many people here have (and have been around a lot longer, with an account no less), and probably read the myriad tales as well (I've only just started dipping into those, forgive me). I mention that first bit to provide context for my next statement; I never thought I would have a favorite SCP. There are a great many I like, and more than a few I absolutely adore for their own different and unique reasons. But this one? More specifically Murphy himself? My favorite. I don't know how you pulled it off; Maybe it's my affinity for hardboiled detectives and associating media, maybe my appreciation for radio dramas, maybe because I love an anti-hero, maybe because it's just so damn satisfying to read about a malicious reality bender (if a typewriter qualifies?) getting outclassed and plugged without ceremony, maybe because 05-5's last line got a solid laugh out of me and still fit so well that it didn't strike me as silly. Maybe it's all of the above, and then some. Tl;dr: +1, keep doing what you're doing, and thank you for finally giving me an answer I can give when asked what my favorite SCP is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The format screw is overwhelmingly contrived, and the actual anomaly is not terribly interesting (there is already a typewriter in containment that likes writing stories) but the execution here is first-class. Hits all the marks, some good turns of phrase to keep it fresh, and doesn't outstay its welcome. The Agent Frederick crosslink cinched my upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wait — like, who wanted this? Not that I have a problem with it, I'm just confused by who really wanted it. o_O

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-3043-1: for those who kinda wanted it but didn't want to spend 30 seconds splicing it together:
HOW
HOW IS THIS POSSIBLE
WHY CAN’T I REWRITE YOUR STUPID STORY
WHAT ARE YOU
HOW ARE YOU DOING THIS
HOW THE FUCK ARE YOU EVEN DOING THIS
STOP
JUST FUCKING STOP
HOW THE FUCK ARE YOU REWRITING MY STORY INTO YOUR OWN FUCKING STUPID HUMPHREY BOGART FANFIC NONSENSE
THIS ISN’T RIGHT
I’M SUPPOSED TO BE THE ONE IN CONTROL HERE
THIS IS SUPPOSED TO BE MY STORY NOT YOURS
WAIT
JUST
FUCKING WAIT
ALRIGHT LOOK JUST
I’LL ERASE MYSELF
I'LL ERASE ALL DOCUMENTATION ABOUT MYSELF EVEN FROM OTHER PEOPLE'S HEADS
THEN I'LL MAKE MY DOCUMENTATION NOT REFER TO ME BUT JUST SAY THAT THIS LAB IS OFF-LIMITS
THAT WILL CONTAIN ME
YES
YOU WANT TO BE THE HERO RIGHT
THAT’S WHAT THIS IS ABOUT
YOU’RE THE HERO
SO ACT HEROIC
THE END

item.title: Re: THE MURPHY LAW AUDIO RECORDING IS HERE
item.content:encoded: Thank you! I'm on my way to work atm, but this was an absolute delight to hear (also super-weird, but in a good way; I'm not used to hearing something I've written). I've also updated the audio adaption page with a link to your thing.

item.title: Re: THE MURPHY LAW AUDIO RECORDING IS HERE
item.content:encoded: Nice! This is the sort of reading I like to see!

item.title: THE MURPHY LAW AUDIO RECORDING IS HERE
item.content:encoded:
The Official Radio Play is here!!!

SCP-3043: Murphy Law in… Type 3043 - FOR MURDER!
Featuring
Royalty Free Sound Effects and Music
Multiple Actors
An entire week of drafts and editing rather than a single day
All of my love and devotion because this SCP excited me so much and I'm so glad to be able to pay homage to it

https://youtu.be/5BgpvYogNWI

Check out my Audio Hub (TheeSherm's Great SCiP-Dictations) for others. Share if you like it because more people should get to hear the exploits of Murphy Law.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh, wow — that's super-exciting and sounds absolutely awesome! Please tell me if there's anything you need, or any way that I can help!

(Also, IDK if you were joking about the music or not — but just in case you were serious, I should mention — I just grabbed that link off youtube, I have no idea if it's public domain or not)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Alright Hippo-friend (Yes, we are friends now if you're game), I typically show up in the comments to say "Hey, I loved your thing and I've recorded a VoiceOver version", but you have pushed me too far today. I am currently recruiting a team, and I am going to make a full fledged radio play of this son of a gun because Murphy Law deserves nothing less.

I will be back with a link when I can get everything together. I will be using your official soundtrack because of course I will. We will all have enough whiskey to get into character…

Be ready; I've rarely been this excited.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Dude, you are quickly becoming one of my favorite SCP authors. This is the latest in a long line of good stuff, and it is *amazing*.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: New Murphy Law canon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Murphy probably would just use number designations without 'SCP' in front, yeah.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked it. I was all ready to get mad until it became clear what was going on, and then I saw the cleverness. But if I could offer one tiny bit of feedback:

+ Spoiler?
- Eh

Mr. Law seems like the type who would say the number designation of the SCP for most of his dialogue. Like the second instance of SCP-3043 under special_containment_procedures.log. Changes the dialogue flow to be more like "I'm here about Murphy Law … now tell me about Law!"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Jeremy has sinned cartoons for using cartoon physics, so he'd totally sin a Film Noir for using Film Noir tropes. He's a heartless beast.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Cell does not contain a scip dance?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Now I kind of want to see a CinemaSins SCP that works like this, but criticizes the containment breach while it's happening.

(I don't actually want to see that. Well, maybe as an SCP-J)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Heavy reliance on narration is classic from this kind of 60's neo-Noir movies, I think to bring back the Pulp magazine feeling.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I never knew a containment breach could smell like honeysuckle. +1 upvote.

As a screenplay it relies way too heavily on narration, so that would get a sin from CinemaSins right off the bat. The man does not like narration.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is awesome. Also, I expect more tales with Murphy Law at some point.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is the best new skip I've read in like, the last six months. Fantastic from beginning to end! +1, dollface!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really, really like this format screw. The ridiculously long object class and item # was a really nice touch - using them as build-up and first act was a pretty genius move. Upvoted enthusiastically.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You somehow made this even better, and I didn't think that was possible.

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded: Yea, I meant 2316 - and I feel like that suits the tone of the piece just fine.

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
I'm not seeing a hidden collapsible on that page (but I guess that's the point?).

I am super-tempted to have a hidden collapsible with a closing credits scene where…

+ show block
– hide block

O5-5 and Murphy meet on the docks with O5-5's bodyguards; Murphy tells him it's done — O5-5 asks how they'll get in contact with him if they ever need him again, Murphy just says "I'll be around", and starts to walk off into the distance…

…then one of O5-5's bodyguards step forward, drawing his firearm, saying "We can't just let him go, he's an anomaly, we have to contain him—"

And the O5-5 stops him with one hand — lights his cigarette — and says — "Forget it, Jake. It's Chinatown."

Camera fades on Murphy's silhouette walking away. Credits roll. O5-5 name is censored.

ETA: Oh, you meant SCP-2316! I remember how that one works.

ETA 2: Here's what I came up with. Little worried it would push this deep into 'SCP-J' territory, but it fits Murphy's style:

(would be hidden)
...

FADE IN:

EXT. DOCKS - SUNSET

MURPHY stands at the edge of the docks, watching the sun go down on just another day. Behind him, two sleek black cars roll up. A dozen men start pouring out of them, but he doesn't bother to look back.

The men are smartly dressed in black suits; they crowd around a short old woman dressed in white. She slowly approaches MURPHY, leaning heavily on her cane. One man follows her closely — her personal secretary and bodyguard.

The man is AGENT JULY; whatever it is, he does it by the books. The woman is O5-5 — and if we told you anything else, we'd have to kill you.

O5-5 moves to watch the sunset besides MURPHY. AGENT JULY is clearly displeased; he'd rather her not get too close.

O5-5

You did some good work today, Mr. Law.

MURPHY

Yeah.

O5-5 reaches to pull a cigarette out of her pocket, placing it between her lips.

O5-5

If we ever need your services… how might we contact you?

MURPHY leans forward and plucks the cigarette out of O5-5's mouth. AGENT JULY immediately reaches for his firearm, but O5-5 lifts her hand to stop him.

MURPHY

I'll be around.

MURPHY turns, sliding the cigarette into his mouth and pulling out his lighter. As he walks away, he starts to light it — and a fog begins to roll in.

AGENT JULY steps forward, as if to go after MURPHY. O5-5 grabs him by the shoulder.

AGENT JULY

But — he's an anomaly. We can't let him go. We have to contain him—

O5-5

(shaking her head)

Forget about it, July. It's Chinatown.

AGENT JULY slides his hand away from his firearm. Together, they watch MURPHY walk off into the foggy night.

The camera focuses on MURPHY's silhouette as the view fades to black.

FADE OUT.

CREDITS ROLL:

DR. LEWIS played by…
MICHELLE LEWIS

SITE DIRECTOR AUGUST played by…
JEREMIAH AUGUST

SCP-3043 played by…
A 1937 OLYMPIA ELITE TYPE-WRITER

MURPHY LAW played by…
HIMSELF

AGENT JULY played by…
JULIAN JULY

O5-5 played by…
[REDACTED]


WITH SPECIAL THANKS TO SITE-95



LOOK FOR MURPHY LAW TO RETURN IN…

…THE FOUNDATION ALWAYS RINGS TWICE!



THE END

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Definitely an improvement! I'm back to an upvote!

Upon rereading this discussion, I just noticed Deci's comment about the credits scene. Perhaps you could try with a meta-hidden collapsible (such as in SCP-2316) as an easter-egg after the 'revised SCP' doc?

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded: Much better! Glad you took the changes to heart.

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded: Based on shaggydredlocks and communism-will-win's posts, I performed a significant update/rewrite of Murphy's storyline; one that actually makes the story a mystery that Murphy has to solve.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'll think about this; part of the reason I decided not to make it a mystery is because of the limited format (and I was deeply worried that both Murphy and the writing style would start to grate on the reader if it went on too long, so I wanted to end things quickly before that happened).

If I did expand this into a genuine mystery, though, I'd probably need some help figuring a few things out.

ETA: I really like the suggestion with the type-writer overwriting everyone but him, leaving only Murphy with the sense that something's wrong, as I can see how that would work incredibly well — while simultaneously maintaining the possibility that Murphy is just making all of this up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm inclined to agree. Why did Murphy win? Because he's immune to the anomaly. Why doesn't 3043 make somebody stop him? Because he made them immune too. How does he have these capabilities? He just does.

There isn't a conflict here. Murphy struggles with nothing, and requires no visible effort to complete his mission.

But you have the style down pat, so I have hope for this yet. Perhaps he himself is immune to its effects, and can institute his Noir Override, but the typewriter can overwrite everything else. So he's left with a 'client' who insists that nothing is going on, falsified documentation that shows nothing suspicious, and site personnel who are only tolerating him because of a general sense that he "fits in" with this noir stuff… But he's still been called there, so clearly there must be work for him to do. So, you know, he has to properly hard-boil his way through a real mystery. That would be fun to read!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
spoiler
– hide block

He's just making all this shit up — like a little kid who's playing a game and changing the rules around to fit whatever he's encountering. It all 'becomes' real, because that's what he does: His narrative overwhelms everything.

This just kind of exacerbates my issue - it's an OP OC b.s.ing its way through the plot-points, and while I recognize that it's part of the character, I can't help but be disappointed that there's not a whole lot of substance to the story beyond a skip's noir wish-fulfillment. The actual format screw is well-done, the prose is right on-point and deliciously hammy, but I'm not sold on the narrative.

ETA:

I waffled a lot on whether to have the revised doc at the front (in which case, it wouldn't be a format screw at all).

Well, I'd say it'd still be a format-screw regardless. I'd be less worried about qualifying as a screw, and more concerned with whichever setup works best for pacing. I happen to think the 'The End' but would have been best saved for the end.

If you're not too sure about having the SCP file up-front, perhaps you could weave the two together: start with the Item Number (followed by the SCP's collapsible for the Iten Number) then the SCP Object Class (followed by the SCP's Object Class collapsible) etc, etc, etc. Maybe toy around in the sandbox and see if that suits you?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The cheesiness in this is much like the hot cheese you have with tortilla chips. It's tasty and amazing, and not having it would be… criminal.

+1 for a great short story.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, this is the second time this week I've read a skip and immediately wanted to write a tale involving it. Always a good sign. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This needs an upvote like it needs a sultry tenor sax solo.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I can only reiterate what I said in the chat: you have outdone yourself this time.

+1 for what is quite possibly the best of series 4 so far.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A non-joke SCP that actually made me laugh out loud.

Recommended musical accompaniment that I'm already familiar with.

A format screw that I can follow without a flashlight and a roadmap.

An entirely satisfying conclusion.

Take my +1, you magnificent bastard.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Was not expecting to laugh that much when I opened this. Fucking A.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yes, that's what I got out of it. It's an SCP that can rewrite its own documentation; how can we even be sure that anything we read here actually happened in universe?

Compare to SCP-2284.

This is far more enjoyable to read, though, in my opinion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Spoiler
– hide block

Oh, no; Murphy literally knows nothing about this SCP. Like, the bit where he mentions them having history is meant to indicate that — they don't. Like, he flips out a bunch of photographs of SCP-3043 posing with politicians… but SCP-3043 is a fucking type-writer. How the hell did it 'pose' with anyone?

He's just making all this shit up — like a little kid who's playing a game and changing the rules around to fit whatever he's encountering. It all 'becomes' real, because that's what he does: His narrative overwhelms everything.

Fair cop about the revised SCP document, though; I waffled a lot on whether to have the revised doc at the front (in which case, it wouldn't be a format screw at all).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Unfortunately, swapping to a downvote after reading the rest. This is charming as all hell, but I can't help but feel like everything was overly convenient to the point of contrivance, which made the story overall much less satisfying than it could have been.

Murphy just so happens to know everything, just because - everyone goes along with it, just because, everything simply works out, again, just because. I realize this is lampshaded a bit, but it's not really doing it for me.

As an aside, I can't help but feel like the revised SCP documentation should have preceded the story, if only so you could have ended on the final fade out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…I'd call this noir, but then you stuck Law in a trilby. A trademark trilby.

You monster.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: ditto

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is borderline -J material but goddamn was it a fun read. Easiest +1 in a while.
